sourceMovie.title: Universal Soldier
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: Audiences latched onto Universal Soldier (1992) as a thriller theatrical experience: big enough for a group watch, specific enough to reward a second viewing once you know where it's headed. The thriller and action blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: Spectral
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Universal Soldier clicked for you, Spectral (2016) is a strong next stop: both trade in thriller and action, with momentum and clean stakes. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Universal Soldier clicked for you, War Machine (2026) is a strong next stop: both trade in thriller and action, with momentum and clean stakes. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

recommendations.title: Edge of Tomorrow
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Universal Soldier clicked for you, Edge of Tomorrow (2014) is a strong next stop: both trade in action and science fiction, with momentum and clean stakes. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after Universal Soldier?
faq.answer: Try Spectral, War Machine, Edge of Tomorrow—each shares DNA with Universal Soldier while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is Universal Soldier?
faq.answer: Universal Soldier (1992) is best described as Thriller, Action, Science Fiction. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is Universal Soldier part of a franchise?
faq.answer: Yes—it's part of the "Universal Soldier Collection" storyline. You can enjoy it standalone, but sequels and tie-ins build on characters and continuity established here.
